reason: The write-enabled Codex companion process exited with status failed (exit 144). Its PRIMARY review pass did run before failing: it read all three deliverables, the primary source, the verl compute_grpo_outcome_advantage, and the group_std baseline, ran the repo leakage detector (clean), and applied substantive edits to context.md/reasoning.md/answer.md (tightened the in-group-mean bias framing to not overclaim exact unbiasedness, added the verl-sample-std vs unbiased=False convention note, fixed fallback-tensor dtype, softened the k3 variance claim). I re-read and re-verified those edits by hand and they are correct. The failure came from the companion erroneously spawning a recursive nested review of itself, which I terminated; that recursion, not the deliverables review, is what produced exit 144. Because the runtime's terminal status was failed, this is marked codex_reviewed:false/failed per the skill rather than completed, even though the deliverables were in fact reviewed-and-fixed and then hand-verified this run.
